entry.title: cannot able to add capabilities what might be the reason
Re: Learning Hub Subscription Purchasing Error (VAL_0101)

Hi @narone,
Once you have send an email to sales@sap.com would you mind to share the ticket number?
This makes it easier to follow up with the respective team.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Brasil!

entry.title: Errors sending queries to google big query
entry.content:
I want to do this simple query to a REMOTE TABLE in GBQ, converting a string to decimal and filtering by timestamp:

SELECT 
ORDER_ID,
CAST(AMOUNT_STRING AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS AMOUNT_DEC,
FROM <GBQ>
WHERE UPDATED_AT_TS > CAST('2024-01-01 00:00:00.000000' as TIMESTAMP)

but it fails.

Why? if I review the query sent to GBQ surprisingly I see:

SELECT 
ORDER_ID,
CAST(TRUNC(AMOUNT_STRING, 2) AS NUMERIC) AS AMOUNT_DEC
FROM <GBQ>
WHERE UPDATED_AT_TS > DATETIME'2024-01-01 00:00:00.000'

Wow.

  • The conversion to decimal 18,2 is in the wrong order, first it tries to leave 2 decimals of a string and then it tries to convert it to a number.
  • The filter in where is invalid, because it is comparing a TIMESTAMP with a DATETIME

Someone should check how type conversions are translated to GBQ because it does not seem to work very well.

PS: I opened an incident about the conversion to decimal, ... and basically I was told that it doesn't work and that's it. And another one about timestamp conversion, which didn't help either. So I'm writing it here in case anyone comes across this.

entry.title: Re: Can you capitalise an asset with depreciation?
entry.content: See KBA 2665996 - "Ordinary depreciation posted" field cannot be changed in T-code: AS92 https://me.sap.com/notes/2665996

entry.title: Re: Combine and SUM query
entry.content:
Hi,

Try this

SELECT T0."ItemCode", T0."ItemName", T0."ItmsGrpCod", T3."ItmsGrpNam", T1."PriceList", 
T2."ListName", T1."Price", sum(T4."OnHand") - sum(T4."IsCommited") + sum(T4."OnOrder") AS "AvailQty"
FROM OITM T0 INNER JOIN ITM1 T1 ON T0."ItemCode" = T1."ItemCode" 
INNER JOIN OPLN T2 ON T1."PriceList" = T2."ListNum" 
INNER JOIN OITB T3 ON T0."ItmsGrpCod" = T3."ItmsGrpCod" 
INNER JOIN OITW T4 ON T0."ItemCode" = T4."ItemCode" 
WHERE T0."ItmsGrpCod" = 126 AND T2."ListNum" = 3
GROUP BY T0."ItemCode", T0."ItemName", T0."ItmsGrpCod", T3."ItmsGrpNam", T1."PriceList", T2."ListName", T1."Price"

Hope this helps,

Son Tran

entry.title: Re: Maximize Customer Retention with Customer Service Tools in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content: Nice article @Luciano_Monteiro, thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: How to Mass update Classification data
entry.content:
You can use CLMM and update your values per material, just like in MM17 for example.

- Open CLMM
- Select the third option: "Assign values in matrix"
- Input your class type and class
- In "Chars", input the characteristics you want to change, and in "Target Objs" input the material numbers you want to change
- Click at the button "Perform mass Change"
- You will see a table with your materials, characteristics and the inputs for adding the new values you want

Now From manage Incident Tile  > People Tab i am unable to add extra details like Address,Phone No,Date of birth of the same person(Create Button is Greyed Out)

Can you please help me on this to get the create button active if any Specific Authorizations/ Configuration Required 

Thanks in Advance

Regards,

Phaneendra

entry.title: Branches in SAP Business One
entry.content:
We are implementing SAP Business One 10 on our local server. These are the 5 different branches -

  1. TNM
  2. Security
  3. Digital
  4. OTS
  5. Digital

Single database and five different branches will be configured in SAP. The branches are in different locations such as Nashik ,Pune and Bengaluru. These are my doubts- 

  1.  If we generate five different companies i.e. five different databases, then is it optimized solution then single database ?
  2. Is cloud based SAP superior then local server because the locations are different ? 
  3. How will we use sap in Bengaluru if server is placed in Nashik ? What will be speed of communication ?
  4. How will we handle common inventory, business partners ? 
  5. If security company leaves the group and forms a independent company  then how to configure it ?
  6. What are the other challenges beyond this ? Some other problems which I can not imagine today ?

KhaingZin

entry.title: Re: iOS Building Error
entry.content:
Hey,

The error message you're encountering suggests that there was an issue while running the command to export and build your iOS app using the SAP AppGyver runtime version. Here are some potential causes and steps to troubleshoot the problem:

  1. Dependency Issues: There might be missing or conflicting dependencies. Ensure that all required dependencies are properly installed. Run yarn install or npm install to make sure all packages are up-to-date.

  2. Template Version: The specific template version @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.11.132 might have issues. Check if there are any updates or known issues with this version.

  3. Xcode and Keychain Issues: The xcodebuildArchiveArgs parameter indicates a keychain issue. Ensure that the specified keychain (AppGyver-84376) is available and accessible. Verify that the keychain is unlocked and that the signing certificates are correctly installed.

  4. Permission Issues: The command might require elevated permissions. Try running the command with sudo to see if it resolves any permission-related issues.

  5. Environment Configuration: Ensure that your development environment is correctly set up. Verify that you have the correct versions of Node.js, npm, yarn, Xcode, and other tools.

  6. Network Issues: Sometimes, network issues can cause package installation failures. Ensure that you have a stable internet connection and try again.

  7. Logs and Error Messages: Check the detailed logs and error messages for more specific information about what might have gone wrong. The logs should provide more context about the failure.

  8. Rebuild: Clean the build artifacts and try rebuilding the app. Sometimes, residual files from previous builds can cause issues. Run commands like npx rnv clean or manually delete the build folders.



    Can maybe check and recheck all the above points. there might be sometimes bugs in sap build **bleep** well. can try to rebuild again. or reconfigure all the authorizations.also can export your project and then remove sap build subscription from marketplace and re subscribe again.

    Thanks,
    Rahul

entry.title: Re: SAP MaxAttention IWS 'Sparking Business Revolution through AI' (Virtual, 25 de Julio de 2024)

entry.title: Registration For SAP TechEd Virtual In 2024 Is Now Live!
entry.content:
It’s time to bring out your best again and join us for this year’s SAP TechEd Virtual! The high-energy, interactive and virtual experience is accessible to everyone and will take place on October 8-9, 2024. Spoiler: Registrants will benefit from an amazing goodie this year – so read on! 

Here’s what you can expect from SAP TechEd Virtual 

SAP TechEd is all about innovation and collaboration. By joining the virtual experience, you can gain the skills and expertise needed to advance your career and help your organization thrive using SAP technologies. Get ready to explore new tech, learn from industry leaders, and discover best practices from SAP experts and partners. Plus: Hear firsthand customer accounts and join a vibrant, growing community of peers. 

Deep dive into 6 SAP TechEd tracks 

At SAP TechEd Virtual, you can choose from six content tracks covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions, each offering a variety of sessions and formats: 

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP 
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General 
  • Application Development and Automation 
  • Data and Analytics 
  • Integration 
  • Artificial Intelligence 

As always, the packed agenda includes keynotes, expert sessions with our Developer Advocates, lectures, and more. The full session catalog will be available on August 15, 2024. 

SAP TechEd goes on tour 

To continue your learning journey beyond SAP TechEd Virtual, you can also participate in one of our in-person SAP TechEd on Tour stops and an additional event hosted by SAPinsider EMEA. 

Get excited for Devtoberfest 

Can’t get enough of SAP TechEd? Then don’t worry, we've got you covered. To extend the spirit of SAP TechEd even further and enable everyone in executing sought-after development skills all year round, Devtoberfest will become Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd and take place September 23 - October 4 and October 14 - 25. Block your calendars today to learn, code, collaborate, and earn the chance to win the grand prize! 

Register now and get an exclusive discount! 

We know that learning is vital – now more than ever. As an SAP TechEd Virtual registrant, you can take advantage of an exciting package of curated learning, engagement, and solution experience offerings, including a 25% discount on SAP Learning Hub. 

Register today for SAP TechEd Virtual, secure your discount and stay tuned for the go-live of the SAP TechEd Learning zone on August 15, where you can acquire in-demand skills and prepare for a globally recognized certification with self-paced learning to boost your career. 

See you all at SAP TechEd Virtual!

Spot anything unusual? 😉

entry.title: Re: Tax Fulfillment Date: What is it and how does it impact reporting?
entry.content:
Dear Ailane,

Thank you for your blog post.

I would like to ask regarding Fulfillment Date derivation at Hungary. There are two customizing tables for this.  Financial Accounting > Financial Accounting Global Settings > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > Tax Fulfillment Date > Define Business Transaction Types/Define Derivation Rules for Business Transaction Types

My questions is where can I find the default derivations for Hungary, how should I fill these two tables by default. Is there a note which provides the appropriate data?
For example I found a note for Poland with some guidance on how to fill the table in some cases: 3357970 - Exchange Rate for Correction Invoice - SLIM VAT 3 - SAP for Me

Is there anything similar for Hungary?

Thank you!
Best regards,
Borbala

I have a requirement for overarching and specific contract information to transfer from the Ariba Contract to any PR/PO that is created in reference to said contract.

This is similar to the functionality in S4/ECC where data from outline agreements transfers to POs at header or item level.

Is it possible to do this in Ariba?

Make sure that "Post to same bin" is off in the WM movement type configuration:

Gostaria de buscar onde posso fazer o treinamento para trabalhar como um consultor de implantação dos módulos CO/ FI. Ouvi dizer que a própria SAP possui uma academia com os cursos e os custos mas não encontrei nem local nem o investimento.

Agradeço antecipadamente!

Jorge Marazzo

entry.title: Created By Column in Exchange Rate History Displays Technical User ID starting with underscore
entry.content:
The Created By column in the Exchange Rate History displays the User ID as Technical User (_XYZ).

Note: _XYZ is the Technical User ID.




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Created By Column in Exchange Rate History Displays T...
entry.content:
You have created a Communication Arrangement for a web service ManageExchangeRateIn and it is assigned to a technical user _XYZ.

The system creates a technical user whenever a communication arrangement is created. The users starting with underscore are for customer communication purposes (B2B / A2X / OData). 

This can be verified by following the below steps:

  • Go to the Application and User Management work center
  • Go to the Communication Arrangements view.
  • Search for the Communication Scenario related to Exchange Rates and click Edit.
  • Navigate to the Technical Data tab to find the User ID.

entry.title: Re: Welcome
entry.content:
I am very much excited to attend this course .

I have just one question/experience sharing  for now - At personal life for my hobby as content creator I use Integration tool like Pabbly

It is super easy to integrate anything everything you name it.

However Enterprise Integrations are not so easy to do till date. Any feedback why it is relatively more complex ?  I did my research and found below , anything to add or different opinion ?

Enterprise integrations like SAP are more complex compared to tools like Pabbly for several reasons:

1. Scale and Scope: SAP systems handle large volumes of data and transactions, requiring robust and scalable integration solutions. The complexity of managing such a scale is significantly higher than for smaller tools.

2. Customization: Enterprise systems like SAP are highly customizable to meet specific business needs. This customization often leads to unique integration requirements that add to the complexity.

3. Security and Compliance: Integrating enterprise systems involves stringent security and compliance requirements. Ensuring secure data exchange and compliance with various regulations adds layers of complexity.

4. Legacy Systems: Many enterprises still use legacy systems that need to be integrated with modern SAP solutions. Bridging the gap between old and new systems can be challenging.

5. Business Processes: Enterprise systems like SAP are deeply embedded in critical business processes. Ensuring seamless integration without disrupting these processes requires careful planning and execution.

6. Data Quality and Consistency: Ensuring data quality and consistency across integrated systems is crucial in enterprise environments. This often involves complex data transformation and validation processes.

7. Technical Expertise: SAP integrations require specialized technical expertise and knowledge of various SAP modules, middleware, and integration platforms like SAP PI/PO, SAP CPI, etc.

These factors contribute to the complexity of integrating enterprise systems like SAP compared to more straightforward tools like Pabbly.

Disclaimer : This question doesn't promote any 3rd party product like Pabbly 

@ShilpaVij @piyush Gakhar @EvaHett 

 

Regards,

Niladri

entry.title: Re: Available-to-promise & Transport Management use diffe...
entry.content:
Hello @tberner 

That is another point where TM is more embedded than integrated. I had a similar situation with SD routes and TM default routes - they have to be consistent to provide smooth integration between sales orders and TM planning. However they are completely independent settings and a custom report had to be developed. 

In my opinion your best bet is a custom enhancement to validate BP data or to synchronize SD and TM receiving hours. I would not count on having that delivered that in standard soon.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Adapt UI for classic applications with Screen Personas: Integral part of the extensibility story
entry.content: Hi - you can move the fields on one tab, across tabs, from tabs to header or vice versa - a position you would like to. Extending the table with custom fields is not possible through the means of Adapt UI for classic apps. For this, you would need to add these fields to the table on the backend already. On the UI layer, adjusting the appearance or moving controls or automate actions is possible. Converting fields to table fields (columns) is not possible on the UI layer itself.

entry.title: Re: HANA Vector Engine and LangChain
entry.content: This implementation unfortunately doesn't work for me. "chat_history" parameter seems to be required in the `invoke` method. Do you have any suggestion how to solve it?

entry.title: Not able to see the batch and batch stock of Split Valuated materials in FSM.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have requirement that the split valuated spare parts need to be consumed via SAP FSM. But we are not able to see batch and batch stock of split valuated spare parts in SAP FSM. 

We have performed the following steps so far....

1. Created Split Valuated Material "SPLITVALTEST" in S/4HANA Public Cloud

2. Maintained Batch Stock for batch "01" and "02" at Technician Storage location (175W) in S/4HANA Public Cloud

3. Used App Name: "Replicate by Object Selection" and used BO type "Product" and "Material Stock" to replicate split valuation material in SAP FSM system

4. In order to display Batch field during material consumption, we set the company setting CoreSystems.BatchManagedItemsEnabled  to TRUE in SAP FSM System

5. While recording a material consumption in SAP FSM, we are getting an error message shown in attachment i.e. "Chosen Item is managed by batch numbers, but there are no batch number available!" 

Please suggest what is missing here.

Thanks, 

Atul Sonawane

entry.title: Re: SAP Manufacturing Execution training
entry.content:
Hi 

I used the script 

// Path for SFCs without Non-Conformances at Current Operation
if(getNCsForCurrentOperation()!=null) {
exit (false);
} else {
exit(true);
}

And the script 

// Path for SFCs with Non-Conformances at Current Operation
if(getNCsForCurrentOperation()!=null) {
exit (true);
} else {
exit(false);
}

I have the same problem as the other scripts. The both different routing script goes to same  "VARNISH" step. Shouldn't we add a value to the formula?

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Resolving duplicate search objects
entry.content:
Hello,

I think List of Known Duplicate Search Objects has some typos:

- CD$ALL~ESH_U_ALLERGEN1~ should be CD$ALL~ESH_U_ALLERGEN~ ?

- for Outbound delivery, it should be <client>~OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_H~ not <client>~INBOUND_DELIVERY_H~

- CD$ALL~ESH_U_PLANEDORDR~s should be CD$ALL~ESH_U_PLANEDORDR~ ?

- SW_CONSTRAINTS_H should be replaced by CD$ALL~ESH_U_CSTRTSW~ ? (missing)

Hi,

The WM movement type system configuration is same as per you mentioned in the Screenshot.

entry.content: Parabéns pelo conteúdo!

entry.title: Integrating Joule with S/4 HANA Private cloud Edition
entry.content:
Prerequisite : Below setup has to be made available for integration.

1. Subscription in BTP global account for Joule, Cloud Identity Service (connectivity plan),SAP Build Workzone,standard edition and a cloud connector.

2. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01 or above accepts the integration.

Steps Overview :

 

1. Subscribing Joule,Cloud Identity Service(Connectivity plan) and SAP Build Workzone to the subaccount.
2. Configure Cloud Connector
3. Configure BTP Destinations 
4. Set up S/4 HANA Cloud Private edition as Content Provider
5. Configure Identity Provisioning to update use-role mapping in Workzone
6. Configure Joule Plugin in a Target Mapping & Assignment to user

1. Subscribing Joule,Cloud Identity Service(Connectivity plan) and SAP Build Workzone to the subaccount 

One BTP subaccount has to be created under global account which will accept all three subscriptions with the region selected having Cloud Foundry enabled

  • Start deploying with Cloud Identity Service connectivity plan after establishing the trust with the subaccount. 

  • Subscribe to SAP Build WorkZone with standard plan as Subscription and Instance

Instance :

Create a key for the the instance:

Subscription :

  • Run the booster setup for Joule selecting the subaccount on which it has to be deployed and select SAP product as S/4 HANA Private Cloud for integration

2. Configure Cloud Connector

Connect your subaccount with the cloud connector and add two Access Control connection with one RFC and one HTTPS protocol in cloud connector

RFC:

Please add below functions for RFC to fetch content from backend system

  • BAPI_USER_GETLIST
  • BAPI_USER_GET_DETAIL
  • PRGN_ACTIVITY_GROUPS_LOAD_RFC
  • PRGN_ROLE_GETLIST

HTTPS :

Please add below resources in cloud connector for HTTPS connection

 

Card manifests and i18n files/sap/bc/lrep/
The UI5 App Index/sap/bc/ui2/app_index/
CDM3 content/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/
Data from OData services/sap/opu/odata/
Data from OData V4 services/sap/opu/odata4/

3. Configure BTP Destinations 

Under BTP subaccount connectivity-->destinations, below destinations has to be created 

  • RFC Destination for IPS to fetch data :

 

FieldValue
NameName of the destination
TypeRFC
DescriptionEnter a description
ProxyTypeOnPremise
UserUserID of your SAP S/4HANA technical service user
PasswordPassword of your SAP S/4HANA  technical service user
Authorization TypeCONFIGURED_USER
Location IDID of the SAP Cloud Connector, if maintained
jco.client.clientClient of your SAP S/4HANA  system
jco.client.mshostVirtual host of your SAP Cloud Connector configuration for Identity Provisioning service integration
jco.client.r3nameSystem ID of your SAP S/4HANA  system
  • Design time destination for content federation :

 

PropertyValue
NameMeaningful name with suffix dt.
TypeHTTP
DescriptionEnter a description
URLThe URL of the Virtual Host of your Cloud Connector: https://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities
Proxy TypeOn-premise
Authentication MethodBasic Authentication.
sap-client (additionalThe client number of the ABAP system. For example: 120
UserTechnical backend user
PasswordBackend user password
  • Runtime destination for content federation :

 

Property

Value
NameMeaningful name with suffix rt.
TypeHTTP
DescriptionEnter a description
URLThe URL of the Virtual Host of the system that is set up in your Cloud Connector.
Proxy TypeOnPremise
Authentication Method Principal Propagation.
HTML5.DynamicDestinationTRUE
sap-platformABAP
sap-clientThe client number of the ABAP system.
sap-sysidSystem ID of the ABAP system
sap-service32 + instance number of ABAP System
  • NavigationService destination for Joule

 

NameNavigationService
TypeHTTP
URLportal-service URL from the service key created for the service instance of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.
Proxy TypeInternet
AuthenticationOAuth2UserTokenExchange
Client IDClient ID from the service key created for the service instance of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.
Client SecretClient Secret from the service key created for the service instance of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.
Token Service URL TypeDedicated
Token Service URLhttps://<uaa url>/oauth/token

Note : Token Service URL,Client Secret,Client ID,URL can be retrieved from the key created for instance of SAP Build Workzone in Step 1.

4.Set up S/4 HANA Cloud Private edition as Content Provider

Login to SAP Build workzone assigning proper roles for the user in BTP user and create one Content provider in the content manager for retrieving the roles from S/4 HANA cloud private system.

 

TitleSAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or any other meaningful name
ID<A unique ID>
Design-Time DestinationSelect the design time destination created in BTP Subaccount ending with dt
Runtime DestinationSelect the runtime destination created in BTP Subaccount ending with rt
Runtime Destination for Dynamic DataThe runtime destination for retrieving dynamic data to display on dynamic tiles. By default, the default runtime destination is used.
Automatically add all content items to subaccountTRUE
Use the Identity Provisioning service to provision user authorizationsTRUE

Expose the PFCG roles from S/4 HANA cloud private using transaction /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE and update content for the Content provider created in this step.

I recommend to navigate to my previous blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-federate-your-abap-pfcg-role-containing-fiori-apps-to-sap-build-work/ba-p/13577722 to explore more about content federation in detail .

5. Configure Identity Provisioning to update use-role mapping in Workzone

Joule provides suggestion in relevance to the roles or authorization present in user's access, So in order to recognize the roles associated with a particular user, IPS would be used to maintain the mapping of user to role in workzone comparing the S/4 HANA backend system.

  • Source system in IPS of ABAP Application Server type

RFC Destination created in step 3 has to be used here in destination name of IPS source system, If destination is not populating and showing  a blank value, Please check for below possibility

  • Cloud Identity Service is not subscribed with Connectivity plan
  • No RFC type destination created in the Subaccount

Transformation logic for source :

{
"user": {
"mappings": [
{
"sourcePath": "$.USERNAME",
"targetVariable": "entityIdSourceSystem"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.USERNAME",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ALIAS.USERALIAS",
"targetPath": "$.externalId",
"optional": true,
"correlationAttribute": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.SAPUSER_UUID.SAP_UID",
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['userUuid']",
"optional": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
"constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.E_MAIL",
"targetPath": "$.emails[0].value",
"optional": true,
"correlationAttribute": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.emails[0].primary",
"condition": "$.ADDRESS.E_MAIL EMPTY false",
"constant": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.emails[0].type",
"condition": "$.ADDRESS.E_MAIL EMPTY false",
"constant": "work"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.FIRSTNAME",
"targetPath": "$.name.givenName",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.LASTNAME",
"targetPath": "$.name.familyName"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.MIDDLENAME",
"targetPath": "$.name.middleName",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.NICKNAME",
"targetPath": "$.nickName",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.TITLE_P",
"targetPath": "$.name.honorificPrefix",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.COUNTRY",
"targetPath": "$.addresses[0].country",
"optional": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.addresses[0].primary",
"condition": "$.ADDRESS.COUNTRY EMPTY false",
"constant": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.addresses[0].type",
"condition": "$.ADDRESS.COUNTRY EMPTY false",
"constant": "work"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.TEL1_NUMBR",
"targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[0].value",
"optional": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[0].primary",
"condition": "$.ADDRESS.TEL1_NUMBR EMPTY false",
"constant": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[0].type",
"condition": "$.ADDRESS.TEL1_NUMBR EMPTY false",
"constant": "work"
},
{
"sourcePaths": ["$.DEFAULTS.LANGU"],
"targetPath": "$.locale",
"valueMappings": [
{
"key": ["W"],
"mappedValue": "bg"
}
],
"optional": true,
"type": "valueMapping",
"defaultValue": "en"
},
{
"sourcePaths": ["$.ADDRESS.LANGUP_ISO"],
"targetPath": "$.preferredLanguage",
"optional": true,
"type": "valueMapping",
"functions": [
{
"function": "toLowerCaseString"
}
]
},
{
"sourcePaths": ["$.LOGONDATA.TZONE"],
"targetPath": "$.timezone",
"valueMappings": [
{
"key": ["EET"],
"mappedValue": "Europe/Sofia"
}
],
"optional": true,
"type": "valueMapping",
"defaultValue": "Europe/Berlin"
},
{
"targetPath": "$.active",
"constant": false
},
{
"targetPath": "$.active",
"condition": "($.ISLOCKED.LOCAL_LOCK != 'L') && ($.ISLOCKED.GLOB_LOCK != 'L') && ($.ISLOCKED.WRNG_LOGON != 'L')",
"constant": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ACTIVITYGROUPS[*].AGR_NAME",
"targetPath": "$.groups[?(@.value)]",
"optional": true,
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
}
]
},
"group": {
"mappings": [
{
"sourcePath": "$.ROLE_NAME",
"targetVariable": "entityIdSourceSystem"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.ROLE_NAME",
"targetPath": "$.displayName",
"functions": [
{
"function": "concatString",
"condition": "'%abap.role.prefix%' !== 'null'",
"prefix": "%abap.role.prefix%"
}
]
},
{
"targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
"constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.USERLIST[*].USERNAME",
"targetPath": "$.members[?(@.value)]",
"optional": true,
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
}
]
}
}
  • Target System in IPS of Workzone type

Select the source system created in above step.

Transformation logic for target:

{
"user": {
"condition": "($.emails EMPTY false)",
"skipOperations": ["update"],
"mappings": [
{
"targetPath": "$.id",
"sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem"
},
{
"targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
"constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User"
},
{
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:2.0:mapping']['providerId']",
"constant": "%cflp.providerId%"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.emails[0].value",
"targetPath": "$.emails[0].value",
"condition": "$.emails[?(@.primary == true)].value == []",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.emails[?(@.primary == true)].value",
"targetPath": "$.emails[0].value",
"condition": "$.emails[?(@.primary == true)].value != []",
"optional": true,
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
"functions": [
{
"function": "elementAt",
"index": 0
}
]
},
{
"targetPath": "$.emails[0].primary",
"condition": "$.emails[0].length() > 0",
"constant": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['userUuid']",
"targetPath": "$.externalId",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.groups[*].value",
"targetPath": "$.groups[?(@.value)]",
"optional": true,
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
"functions": [
{
"function": "resolveEntityIds",
"entityType": "group"
}
]
}
]
},
"group": {
"mappings": [
{
"targetPath": "$.id",
"sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem"
},
{
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:2.0:mapping']['providerId']",
"constant": "%cflp.providerId%"
},
{
"targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
"constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group"
},
{
"targetPath": "$.schemas[1]",
"constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:mapping",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.displayName",
"targetPath": "$.externalId"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.externalId",
"targetPath": "$.externalId",
"optional": true,
"functions": [
{
"function": "replaceAllString",
"regex": "(?i)(^pcd:)",
"replacement": ""
},
{
"function": "replaceString",
"target": "/",
"replacement": ":"
},
{
"function": "replaceString",
"target": "(",
"replacement": "@"
},
{
"function": "replaceString",
"target": ")",
"replacement": "+"
}
]
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.members[*].value",
"targetPath": "$.members[?(@.value)]",
"optional": true,
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
"functions": [
{
"function": "resolveEntityIds"
}
]
}
]
}
}

Properties for target system.

AuthenticationBasicAuthentication
cflp.group.unique.attributeexternalId,['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:2.0:mapping']['providerId']
cflp.patch.group.members.above.threshold5000
cflp.providerId<ID of the content provider you created in your SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition tenant
cflp.user.unique.attributeemails[0].value,['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:2.0:mapping']['providerId'],externalId
ips.trace.failed.entity.contentFALSE
OAuth2TokenServiceURL<Value of the url property of the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition service key you created + /oauth/token>
Password<Value of the clientsecret property of the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition service key>
ProxyTypeInternet
TypeHTTP
URL<Value of the portal-service property of the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition service key>
User<Value of the clientid property of the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition service key service key>

 Now run the sync job from source system and check the mapping available in Identity provisioning tab in Settings tab of workzone. This will bring the role mapping of users in the repository

6. Configure Joule Plugin in a Target Mapping & Assignment to user

Login to S/4 HANA system and execute transaction /n/ui2/flpd_cust (launchpad designer) and create a custom tile adding below mentioned details and add the catalog to a role and assign to your user.

 

Semantic ObjectShell
Actionplugin
Application TypeSAP UI5 Fiori App
TitleJoule.
URLURL of the Joule application appended with /resources/public/webclient/s4.
IDsap.das.webclientplugin.s4

 

Now launch your S/4 HANA Fiori launchpad to get the Joule and access it with your Identity Authentication Service credentials, If SAML2 is enabled with the same IAS tenant then SSO will work.

Conclusion

These steps completes the integration of Joule with S/4 HANA Private cloud with a navigational response according to user's authorization.

References

https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/integrating-joule-with-sap/introduction

entry.title: Re: Excited to be a part of the community
entry.content:
Hi @AchyuthNateri ,

Welcome aboard! Please let me know if you have any questions about your learning journey.

Is a significant amount of your studies centered around SAP specifically?

entry.title: Re: Simplifying HTML5 Content Consumption Across Subaccounts
entry.content:
Hi @rupambhatta, 

Thanks for sharing this key feature for BTP! 

One question: how the user identity from the consumer subaccount (Build WorkZone) will be propagated to the provider subaccount (MTA with HTML5)? If the HTML5 application is protected by authentication and authorization (role collections), how it will be propagated and checked in the consumer subaccount?

Regards,

Fabiano Rosa

entry.title: Re: Rework Activities in Flexible Workflows
entry.content:
@SBach wrote:

Soon additionally, you can overwrite that configuration (either the standard config or the one developed in the callback) for a specific step, in SWDD_SCENARIO (Important note: this is actual not available so an outlook in 2020!!!)

I'm wondering if I overwritten any standard config in flexible workflow block, will it be reverted back to original upon next S/4 upgrade?

I'm trying to replace standard callback class with my own custom callback class in standard template Approve Purchase Requisition Header Scenario (WS02000458).

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: @bglobee what a great story! I agree with you no looking back. I worked in supply chain many years ago and we implemented SAP. So, I started as an end user before joining SAP. Many years later I have held many positions and still use SAP everyday in some capacity - SFSF, Concur, Ariba, Analytics Cloud, you name it. How far we (and so many have come.) May I ask what learning journey you are taking or planning to take? You should meet SAP Champions too - @KatherineKenneally will tell you who they are!!

entry.title: Re: delete cost center group
entry.content: thank you

entry.title: Could you please reactivate open sap course "SAP Cloud Platform Version Control with Git"?
entry.content: The open sap course "SAP Cloud Platform Version Control with Git" was a great introduction to the learning of Git. Although it was based on git usage over SAP Web IDE, the fundamentals of Git were greatly introduced by Arnaldo Cavazos. Please, reactivate open sap course "SAP Cloud Platform Version Control with Git".

entry.title: Re: Can the system make a beep/bell sound when there is a...
entry.content: This is great.... the audio file could also be stored with Resources and pulled from there.

entry.title: Re: Configure Process Visibility Dashboard & Integration with Fiori Launchpad: Part 7
entry.content:
awesome starting inputs 

Query is "How we can put down filters narrowing the data that displays based on the person logged in"
For ex: there are 5 requests, 2 each from USER1, USER2 and 1 from USER3

Now when USER1 opens monitoring section of visibility scenario, he/she should only see 2 requests from USER1.

Similarly for other users.

Thanks in advance for your valuable inputs!!!

entry.title: Re: Deploying app from VS code gets error 403
entry.content: Hi, I faced an issue as you can see my question in my profile but today I got other issue with error 403. I am using BAS, is the solution the same? If yes, how can I add the service? Thank you

entry.title: Re: openSAP has moved: Find over 100 former openSAP courses on the SAP Learning site
entry.content:
@AnnikaLink I don't see Record of Achievement nor Confirmation of Participation provided for openSAP Courses anymore in these migrated courses. 

From Learning point of view , it's fantastic but Young Modern Learners look for motivational element too - so providing a Digital Badge/ Record of of Achievement /Confirmation of Participation will definitely increase usage of these content.

entry.title: Re: Quiz: incorrect number of selections
entry.content: This looks like some bug. Can you reload the course and open up the quiz again? Then check if it allows you to select any answers.

entry.title: Re: Database Vendor Code: 1427 Error code:-2147215357 [CR...
entry.content:
If it works in one but not the other it's likely environmental issue.

Make sure you are using the same Oracle Client on UAT system.

Check permissions, CR fully supports security, try a different user like the Admin account to see if that works.

And can you remove the SDK Tag, it's not the right one to use.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Future SAP consultant here just expressing my gratitude and interest in the digital skills initiative program!  I've used a lot of MRP/ERP systems throughout my career but SAP is by far the one I prefer to work in.  Thanks for all the useful information @David_Chaviano .

I hit a few of the guidelines for the underrepresented candidates for this program:

  • Military veterans - former Intelligence Analyst
  • Single parent - 2 kids
  • Diagnosed with ADD 4 years ago (drastically helped learning to cope with structure and etc)
  • Geographically limited to career opportunities - with shared custody of my kids there are limited options within a valid commute and remote options are very competitive 
  • First generation college students - first one in my immediate family to pursue a college degree.  Looking to supplement the SAP certifications with a degree in Information Systems
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life - Looking to use the knowledge I've gained in the MFG industry and going through a few implementations to lead me to a career in SAP Consulting.

All in all I've been through ~5 SAP implementations and/or cleanups.  I've worked with cross functional partners to make sure our design would work for all parties involved and worked directly with the consultants and used past SAP setups to come up with a solution that would work for our current needs.

Thanks again!

entry.title: SAP Inside Track PORTO 2023 - My experience as a speaker
entry.content:
Topic : SAP Integration Suite Overview and real world scenarios .

Author: Shaik Azmathulla | SME (Integration Suite | PO) | Integration Black belter

Greetings, Community Cohort!

"I'm thrilled to share that I had the opportunity to present as a speaker at SAP Inside Track Brazil on October 25, 2023, discussing 'SAP BTP Integration Suite overview and real-world scenarios'. This engaging experience took place through an online platform, making it both an outstanding event and a great learning journey.

My session was focused mainly on the key takeaways below.

  • SAP BTP Integration Overview.
  • SAP’s Integration history and investment.
  • Challenges as a customer to accelerate business outcomes with simplified integration.
  • Hybrid integration strategy.
  • Use cases.

SAP BTP Integration Suite: The SAP BTP Integration Suite is the strategic integration service within SAP BTP. It facilitates the integration of business processes that span different departments, organizations, or companies. It acts as a middleware between the systems that can relate to each other. The integration service then arranges for the technical communication between the components and the correct "translation" of data.

SAP Integration Suite Introduction: SAP Integration Suite represents a cutting-edge integration platform as a service (iPaaS) solution. Integration serves as the cornerstone of building an intelligent enterprise, emerging as the primary strategic focus for organizations worldwide. By seamlessly linking customer, product, employee, and brand experiences with business operations in real-time and continuous fashion, organizations can unlock the complete potential of their system landscape. This empowers them to make informed decisions precisely when needed, leveraging the rich insights derived from interconnected data sources and processes.

 

Integration Suite Capabilities:

Cloud Integration: Cloud integration refers to the process of configuring multiple cloud-based systems to connect and work together seamlessly. It involves combining different cloud services and on-premises systems to function as a cohesive unit, allowing data and processes to be shared and managed across various applications and platforms.

API Management: Get access to simple, scalable, and secure digital assets through application programming interfaces (APIs) and consume these.

Open Connectors: Build seamless integrations with over 160 non-SAP applications using prebuilt connectors.

Integration Advisor: Accelerate the development of business-oriented interfaces and mappings, generate runtime artifacts quickly, and significantly reduce efforts.

Trading Partner management: Manage B2B relationships with multiple trading partners through this easy-to-use user interface that helps simplify the B2B communication between trading partners.

Integration Assessment: Define an integration landscape, based on the Integration Solution Advisor Methodology.

Migration Assessment: Assess your existing integration scenarios to migrate them to SAP Integration Suite.

SAP’s Integration history and investment.

SAP (Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing) has a long history of evolving its integration capabilities to meet the needs of businesses worldwide. Integration is becoming a key role in business scenarios. It started with XI in 2003 and continuous investment till now and come up with SAP Integration Suite.

Hybrid integration strategy:

SAP’s integration strategy focuses on providing seamless connectivity and interoperability between SAP and non-SAP systems, both on-premises and in the cloud. The strategy emphasizes flexibility, scalability, and simplicity to meet the diverse needs of modern enterprises. Here are the key elements of SAP’s integration strategy.

Integration Principles: 4 key principles are Cloud Integration, API Management, Open Connectors, and Integration Advisor.

Integration Platform: Versatile, enterprise-grade hybrid integration platform – SAP Integration Suite for holistic integration.

Integration Methodology: SAP Integration Solution Advisory methodology – to guide customers to choose the right integration approaches and tools.

Based on the different scenarios, the following table shows the integration tools selected.

Scenarios

Integration tools

Cloud to Cloud

CI / Other capabilities from Integration Suite

Cloud to on premise

CI / Other capabilities from Integration Suite

On premise to Cloud

CI / Other capabilities from Integration Suite

On premise to on premise

Edge Integration cell

Please find the following link for the rest of the information and demos.

SAP BTP Integration Suite overview and real-world scenarios: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QsrTPaQc1W8

Recognition: I cherish the wonderful memories of the event and the certificate I received at the end.

My sincere thanks to #sapcommunity , I am grateful for this great opportunity.

Thankyou so much @MRobalinho for providing this oppurnity . 

Finally, I would like to thank organizers, and sponsors. It won’t be possible to achieve success without a team.

Upon completion, I am eagerly anticipating the SAP Fortaleza 2024 event.

Best Regards,

Azmath.

entry.title: Advanced Variant Configuration_S/4HANA Public cloud_finished goods stock with material variant
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We have a requirement to view Finished Goods (configurable material with material variant) stock report in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. Please advise whether any Standard API or CDS view is available in SAP Standard to link the material variant to stock (Finished Goods with material variant)

In Stock overview Fiori app, we can only view the stock attached to the sales order but material variant is not linked to stock in SAP S/4HANA public cloud. Please refer to below screenshot. 

Please advise. 

Thank you,

kind regards

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Re: Sloc to sloc against the bin to bin transfer
entry.content:
Hi,

Pls find the Screenshots of PCN and TO confirmation.

entry.title: error when downloading data from SAP universe to lumira
Re: BI content activation problem "A previous activation is still running"
entry.content: Services & Support/ KBAs & Notes/ Controlling/ Overhead Cost Controlling/ Cost Center Accounting (CO-OM-CCA) 2618606 - Status "Activation in Progress" while activating /ERP/SFIN_PLANNING

entry.title: Congratulations to the Winners of ULatinHack 2024 & video recap
entry.content:
¡Anunciamos con orgullo a los ganadores de ULatinHack! 🏆🎉

Felicitamos a todos los equipos por sus increíbles proyectos y dedicación. Los ganadores de esta edición han demostrado una creatividad y talento excepcionales, creando soluciones innovadoras que tendrán un gran impacto en el sector de la salud.

🏅 Primer lugar: BIOHACKERS - Universidad de Los Andes
🥈 Segundo lugar: DigitAlholics - Universidad Peruana de Ciencias Aplicadas
🥉 Tercer lugar: AndesLand - Universidad de Los Andes

Gracias a todos los participantes, mentores y sponsors por hacer de ULatinHack un evento inolvidable. ¡Sigamos creando juntos el futuro! 🚀💡

#ULatinHack #ULatinHack2024 #Innovación #Tecnología #Salud

Para ver el video de recap del evento por favor consultar este post de linkedin 

Para ver las fotos del equipo ganador y de los ponentes en el evento final ver este post de linkedin

Hasta la próxima edición, entonces! 

SAP University Alliances

entry.title: how we can fetch first and last day of a given month by referring incoming payload
entry.content:
Hi All,

i hope you're doing well.

i have a requirement that from source we will get 3 letter of a month , by that we need make calucate first and last day of the month in the format yyyyMMdd . i have tried multiple way but no luck. can you please here.

source we will get in XML format.

Thanks in-advance.

BR,

Reddy

entry.title: TCS Postings in FB01/FB70
entry.content:
Is it possible to post TCS (TAX COLLECTED AT SOURCE)related entries in FB01/FB70?

Ex-

Customer A/c DR 1000

To Revenue A/c CR 800

To Output GST A/C CR 150

To TCS A/x CR 50

entry.title: SAP trainings _previous work in SAP GUI (AP, CAP) +Tester
entry.content:
I am currently a UAT test analyst looking to improve/develop/enhance my skills in SAP field (maybe as an SAP consultant).

With over 5 years of experience in AP and Project Controlling in SAP GUI, I am currently involved in a transformation project from SAP ERP to SAP HANA until 2027/2028 within my employer, Allianz . During this project, I have gained experience in testing phases and managing business processes within feeders and SAP processes.

Could you recommend suitable training courses to enhance our existing knowledge in Testing and SAP Fiori/Hanna?

I have a budget of approximately 1400 EUR for courses. I am looking for paid courses to make the best use of this budget.

Thank you very much !

entry.title: Re: Combine and SUM query
entry.content: Son Tran, you're awesome! That works perfectly, thank you so much!

entry.title: Re: SAP Manufacturing Execution training
entry.content: Maybe it depends on NC Code setup. I tested in the latest patch of ME 15.5.

entry.title: Plan Data Upload Starter - Directly upload plan data from a local Excel or CSV file via Story
entry.content:
Overview

As part of the SAC 2023 Q3 release, Planning users can now use the native story trigger to upload flat files directly from story. It will allow the end users to mass upload the data directly from story without the need to go through the modeler.

  • Administrators/Process Owners can define a dedicated end-user data upload job from within the modeler and can upload a file to act as the template structure.

  • Story designers will have access to a dedicated 'data import' planning trigger (similar to data actions). Within the widget they will reference the relevant model and data upload job (described above), in addition to other default settings (e.g., target version, automatic publish).

  • At execution time (i.e., on trigger click), end-users will be prompted to select a source file for upload as well as the target version (if made available by the story designer)

Below is a step-by-step guide on how to enable the upload job and add it into a story.

  • Admin Users can create the upload job with the desired upload template which can be shared with the end users.

  • End users can prepare the data in the template format and upload it directly from the story using the Upload Job Starter.

Pre-requisites: This will be supported only for the new model, on non-SAP data centre tenants.

 

Step 1: Create a Upload Job

Modelers/Admin can now see a Upload Jobs Section under the Data Management Tab along with Import and Export Jobs. Click on the Upload Data button present on the right side to upload the template.

 Source Template File.

 

 Enter the upload job name and click on select source file to select the upload template.  (.csv/.xlsx and .txt are supported). Select the delimiter. Click on Create.

 

Click on Set up Upload.Similar to an Import Job, The Modeler can create necessary transformations to align it with Model Master and Transaction data.

Map to Model Dimension and Measures

 

Validate and Click on Finish to Set up the Upload Job.

 

Click create to complete.

 

Step 2: Add the Upload Job Button to the Story.

Open the story you want to add the Upload Job Starter.Upload Starter Job Widget will be added similar to the Data Action widget.

 

Provide the necessary details

  1.  Name and Description.
  2. Select the desired Model.
  3. Select the Upload Job created in Step 1 as highlighted below.
  4. Select remaining settings like 'Use Recommended Planning Area' or Publish Settings after Execution.
  5. Admin can also fix the version or set it as prompt to be selected by end user.
  6. Once completed ,save the story.

 

Step 3: Upload the File.

On opening the story, the end user can view the widget and use it upload the data. Click on 'Sales Plan Upload' Button.

 

End user will be prompted to select the flat file and version. Make the desired selections and click on Run.

 

Once Completed, user will get a message that the job is completed.

 

In case of any rejects , end user can download the rejected data and correct the entries and run the upload again.

 

Upload activity can also be viewed in the Data management Tab

Upload Activity is also visible in the Activity Log to track the updates made by the end users.

Summary

Upload Job Starter can be very useful to upload mass data flat files by the end users without the need of going to the modeler or transformation. It can be a useful addition to plan data uploads along with data entry.

Hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: RAP Framework - Custom Entities GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET
entry.content: Hi Andre/Junwu!

entry.title: Re: Signavio Security
entry.content: Thank you very much for your answer and help. Kind Regards

entry.title: Re: Is there an API Endpoint to attach a file to a specif...
entry.content: Remember when doing upload via postman, it is important to pass to file as form-data in body, i tried to send this as raw and it didn't work. This note can help as well https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3417551

entry.title: Re: RAP Framework - Custom Entities GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET
entry.content: Thanks for the answer! So it seems that the only way to handle this is to retrieve the header data again when fetching the items, right? I can't think of another way to pass data between sessions except for shared memory, which we can't use in Public Cloud I believe.

entry.title: Re: com.crystaldecisions.sdk.exception.SDKServerException: Internal error.
entry.content: use the jar with name corbaidl jar that comes with bo installation

entry.title: Re: com.crystaldecisions.sdk.exception.SDKServerException: Internal error.
entry.content: because this jar contains oca_abuse class

entry.title: Re: SAC - Maintain Time Dimensions: Hierarchy Not Showing
entry.content: Deleted

entry.title: Deployment has failed. Request failed with status code 403
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I face this issue.

I followed this     https://github.com/SAP/open-ux-tools/tree/main/packages/deploy-tooling#configuration-with-logging-enabled   but still have the error. Yesterday was the error status code 503, today without changing anything I got status code 403.

 

Have someone any idea? With detailed explanation please.

 

Thank you

Mahmood

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Application studio not loading
entry.content: i am facing the same issue .I created a btp account and now want to create BAS account but it is not creating and throwing error

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Get Value by Data Action
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I have a scenario where I have a dummy data row called "Holder," and I want to copy its values to valid data rows. Please see the table below:

Current State     
Dimension 1Dimension 2 Dimension 3 Dimension 4 Dimension 5 Amount (Measure)
      
Holder####150.000
A1B1C1D1E1 
A2B2C2D2E2 
A3B3C3D3E3 
A4B4C4D4E4 
      
      
Desired Outcome    
      
Dimension 1Dimension 2 Dimension 3 Dimension 4 Dimension 5 Amount (Measure)
      
Holder####150.000
A1B1C1D1E1150.000
A2B2C2D2E2150.000
A3B3C3D3E3150.000
A4B4C4D4E4150.000

I've tried various data action coding techniques but haven't achieved the desired outcome. When I use DATA(Dim1=A1)=Resultlookup(Dim1=Holder, Dim2=#), it generates a dataset for every available master data entry. On the other hand, using FOREACH.Booked doesn't work either, as the master data is used in other data rows as well and therefore generates again unvalid data rows

Does anyone have any suggestions?

@N1kh1l

entry.title: Re: SAC - Maintain Time Dimensions: Hierarchy Not Showing
entry.content: Been figuring out time based calculation views for days now as well. This article is really helpful. However, after creating the time-based calculcation view, the view still does not pop up in the SAC as a way to add a time-based dimension to my live data model coming from the same HDI container in SAP HANA. Any hints on how to access the time dimensions in the SAC? (Ultimately, we want to be able to drill down into date hierarchies in a SAC story.)

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: @bglobee @StephanieMarley - absolutely, everyone is welcome to come and connect with our SAP Champions, meet them in our directory and check out their expertise!

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Get Value by Data Action
entry.content:
@guenay 

What is the definition of valid combination. How will SAC know if the combination is valid.

 

Nikhil
